title: Why-First Report: AMV Actuation Timeout Diagnostics
outcome: synthetic command clipping improved, but both matched candidates still timed out with route progress effectively blocked

mechanism_activation.name: synthetic_amv_actuation_feasibility
mechanism_activation.evidence: Issue #2443 shows command clipping fell from 22 to 15 steps, but Issue #2440 closes the timeout driver as route_task_progress_blocked_after_feasibility_improvement

failure_mechanism.label: route_task_progress_blocked_after_feasibility_improvement
failure_mechanism.rationale: Reduced clipping and zero yaw saturation did not move final route progress or success, so command feasibility was not the immediate completion blocker on this slice

paired_comparator.name: hybrid_rule_v3_fast_progress_baseline
paired_comparator.outcome: baseline and actuation-aware intervention both ended timeout_low_progress with nearly identical final route progress

alternative_explanations: Raw simulation_trace_export.v1 frame and event IDs are unavailable in the compact artifacts
Deadlock and oscillation detectors are unavailable, so unrelated navigation deadlock cannot be fully ruled out
Speed-cap binding and scoring conservativeness are only partially instrumented and are not selected as primary explanations

decision.rationale: Actuation-aware scoring should not be broadened as a planner-improvement mechanism from this slice because feasibility improved without route-progress or success movement
decision.next_step: Investigate route-progress geometry or task-completion blockers, or use #2446 only to assess actuation feasibility as a diagnostic ranking dimension
